predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the U.S. Senate Banking Committee will hold a public confirmation hearing for Kevin Warsh for a Federal Reserve leadership role (Chair, Vice Chair, or Governor).
predictions.verification_criteria: Senate Banking Committee publishes an official hearing notice and/or archived webcast showing a confirmation hearing where Warsh testifies for the specified Fed role on or before 2026-06-30.
evidence: Warsh hearing has been delayed, increasing uncertainty but keeping the process active (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/10/warsh-fed-nomination-hearing-delayed.html).
monitoring_signals: Senate Banking Committee hearing schedule updates
Congress.gov nomination tracking for Warsh
Press releases from Senate Banking Committee leadership
predictions.rationale: A delayed hearing suggests scheduling friction, but nominees for high-profile roles typically receive a hearing within weeks to months if the nomination remains live.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, the FOMC will not reduce the target range for the federal funds rate compared with the target range in effect on 2026-04-09.
predictions.verification_criteria: All FOMC statements (including any intermeeting action) through 2026-07-31 show the target range is unchanged versus the range in effect on 2026-04-09.
evidence: Core inflation remains sticky around 3%, complicating near-term easing (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/09/core-inflation-was-3percent-in-february-as-expected-key-fed-gauge-shows.html).
Conflict-driven energy shocks risk second-round inflation effects, raising the bar for early cuts (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/10/oil-price-wti-brent-saudi-pipeline-attack-middle-east-war.html).
monitoring_signals: FOMC meeting statements and press conferences
CME FedWatch probabilities for June/July meetings
Monthly core PCE / CPI releases
predictions.rationale: Sticky underlying inflation plus war-related energy uncertainty makes near-term cuts less likely even if cuts remain plausible later in 2026.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the CBOE Crude Oil Volatility Index (OVX) will close at or above 70.0 on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: CBOE OVX official close (end-of-day value) is ≥ 70.0 on any trading day on or before 2026-06-30.
evidence: Hormuz traffic not normalizing keeps risk premia embedded in crude pricing (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/10/oil-price-wti-brent-saudi-pipeline-attack-middle-east-war.html).

Iran chokepoint leverage via potential tolls/fees could amplify uncertainty in transport costs (https://www.marketwatch.com/story/heres-how-much-money-iran-can-make-charging-tolls-on-one-fifth-of-the-worlds-seaborne-oil-46c05a8b).
monitoring_signals: Daily OVX closes from CBOE/market data terminals
Large moves in front-month Brent/WTI futures
UKMTO/CMF incident alerts affecting Gulf shipping
predictions.rationale: Geopolitical supply and transit risks often express first in implied volatility; OVX spikes are plausible if incidents recur.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, AAA’s U.S. national average retail price for regular gasoline will be at or above $4.25 per gallon on at least one day.
predictions.verification_criteria: AAA Gas Prices daily U.S. national average for regular gasoline reaches ≥ $4.25/gal on any date on or before 2026-06-30.
evidence: Oil remains supported as Gulf shipping risk persists (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/10/oil-price-wti-brent-saudi-pipeline-attack-middle-east-war.html).
Attacks on Saudi infrastructure highlight renewed supply-shock risk (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/09/iran-war-oil-saudi-arabia-east-west-pipeline.html).
monitoring_signals: AAA Gas Prices national average series
EIA weekly gasoline price and inventory reports
Refinery utilization and crack spread indicators
predictions.rationale: Sustained crude risk premia and refinery constraints can quickly transmit to U.S. pump prices, especially into summer demand.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, the U.S. Department of Energy will announce an emergency release/sale from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve totaling at least 15 million barrels (single announcement or cumulative within one announced action).
predictions.verification_criteria: DOE press release (or official DOE SPR announcement) on or before 2026-07-31 specifies a release/sale of ≥15,000,000 barrels due to market disruption/emergency conditions.
evidence: Conflict-driven oil transit and infrastructure risks are supporting prices and could trigger policy responses (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/09/iran-war-oil-strait-hormuz-tanker-ship-uae.html).
Saudi production disruption underscores supply vulnerability (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/09/iran-war-oil-saudi-arabia-east-west-pipeline.html).
monitoring_signals: DOE press releases and SPR announcements
White House statements on fuel prices
EIA weekly petroleum status report (inventories, imports)
predictions.rationale: An SPR action is a common tool to counter acute price spikes or supply disruptions, but political and inventory constraints reduce probability.

predictions.text: By 2026-09-15, the FAO Food Price Index value for any month in 2026 (through August 2026) will be at least 10% higher year-over-year versus the same month in 2025.
predictions.verification_criteria: FAO’s published Food Price Index (nominal index value) for at least one month from Jan–Aug 2026 is ≥ 1.10 × the index value for the corresponding month in 2025, based on FAO’s official release tables.
evidence: El Niño risk plus conflict-driven fertilizer and shipping disruptions could lift global food prices (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/09/el-nino-food-risks-iran-war-fertilizer-weather.html).
monitoring_signals: Monthly FAO Food Price Index releases
Global fertilizer benchmark prices (urea, DAP, potash)
Freight/insurance surcharges for Middle East routes
predictions.rationale: Food inflation requires multiple channels (weather + inputs + logistics); risk is meaningful but timing and magnitude are uncertain.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the S&P 500 will experience at least one single-day close-to-close decline of 3.5% or more.
predictions.verification_criteria: Using official S&P 500 index closes, there exists at least one trading day on or before 2026-06-30 where (Close_t / Close_{t-1} − 1) ≤ −0.035.
evidence: Oil and shipping-risk uncertainty remains embedded post-cease-fire, elevating macro and market volatility (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/10/oil-price-wti-brent-saudi-pipeline-attack-middle-east-war.html).
Sticky inflation complicates the rates outlook, increasing risk of risk-asset repricing (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/09/core-inflation-was-3percent-in-february-as-expected-key-fed-gauge-shows.html).
monitoring_signals: VIX level and term structure
Daily Brent/WTI price gaps on conflict headlines
Credit spreads (HY OAS) widening
predictions.rationale: Geopolitical shocks and rate uncertainty increase tail-risk of a large down day even without a sustained bear market.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, at least one Gulf state (UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, or Saudi Arabia) will announce a full temporary closure of its national airspace lasting at least 12 consecutive hours, explicitly citing military/security risk.
predictions.verification_criteria: An official aviation authority statement and/or NOTAMs show complete suspension of civilian overflight/operations for ≥12 consecutive hours, with public attribution to military/security risk, on or before 2026-06-30.
evidence: Cease-fire appears fragile and regional military risk remains elevated around the Gulf (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/09/iran-war-oil-strait-hormuz-tanker-ship-uae.html).
monitoring_signals: NOTAM feeds and national civil aviation authority announcements
EASA/FAA conflict-zone advisories
Flight tracking deviations and mass cancellations at Gulf hubs
predictions.rationale: Airspace closures are extreme but have precedent during acute escalation; probability is limited by high economic cost and de-escalation incentives.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the EU will adopt (via Council Decision/Regulation published in the Official Journal) a new Iran-related sanctions update that lists at least one shipping or maritime-logistics entity and at least five specific vessels (by name or IMO number).
predictions.verification_criteria: Official Journal of the European Union publication on or before 2026-12-31 includes an Iran-sanctions listing update that (a) adds at least one maritime/shipping entity and (b) identifies ≥5 vessels by name or IMO number in annexes.
evidence: Hormuz transit leverage and persistent shipping risk raise pressure for maritime-focused policy responses (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/09/iran-war-oil-strait-hormuz-tanker-ship-uae.html).

Potential Iranian tolling/monetization of the chokepoint would increase incentives to target maritime networks (https://www.marketwatch.com/story/heres-how-much-money-iran-can-make-charging-tolls-on-one-fifth-of-the-worlds-seaborne-oil-46c05a8b).
monitoring_signals: EU Council press releases on Iran restrictive measures
Official Journal updates to Iran sanctions annexes
Announcements by EU member states on vessel seizures/designations
predictions.rationale: If tensions persist, EU packages often expand to maritime facilitation networks; vessel-level listings are a common enforcement mechanism.
